feed.title: Daniel Roy Greenfeld
feed.subtitle: Inside the head of Daniel Roy Greenfeld
feed.rights: All rights reserved 2024, Daniel Roy Greenfeld

entry.title: TIL: Writing decorators for classes
entry.summary: To my surprise writing decorators for classes is easier than for functions.
entry.content:
To my surprise writing decorators for classes is easier than for functions. Here's how to do it with an arguably wholly unnecessary decorator.

def tools(cls):
    def simplistic_attribute_count(self):
        """Returns the number of attributes."""
        return len(self.__dict__)

    def docs(self: object):
        """Returns the docstring for the class."""
        return self.__doc__

    cls.simplistic_attribute_count = simplistic_attribute_count
    cls.docs = docs
    return cls

Let's test it out:

@tools
class A:
    """A simple class for testing the tools decorator"""


a = A()
a.one = 1

assert a.simplistic_attribute_count() == 1
assert a.docs() == 'A simple class for testing the tools decorator'
